## Summary

<!-- Describe what this PR changes and why. -->

## Type of Change

- [ ] `fix` — Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [ ] `feat` — New feature (non-breaking)
- [ ] `feat!` — Breaking change (existing callers must update)
- [ ] `chore` — Maintenance (deps, tooling)
- [ ] `docs` — Documentation only

## Checklist

- [ ] I followed [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format
- [ ] All new inputs have descriptions and defaults in the workflow `inputs:` block
- [ ] README.md updated if new inputs/outputs were added
- [ ] Breaking changes are documented with `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer
- [ ] I tested the change against a real caller project (link the run below)
